ksa_text: Skill in influencing organizational decision-makers and cross-functional stakeholders to adopt data-driven approaches, including framing technical value propositions in business terms, building consensus across competing priorities, and overcoming resistance through evidence rather than authority.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting reviews of technical systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

Skill in conducting reviews of technical systems.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Leadership & Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the AI CoE Leader. This skill KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (c suite).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Leadership & Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.463). External reviewers may question whether this specific skill KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the AI CoE Leader versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: McKinsey, Gartner, and Harvard Business Review consistently identify leadership and strategy skills as core differentiators for C-suite and VP data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Leadership & Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for AI CoE Leader.

decision_rationale: Leadership & Strategy skill KSA is within scope for the AI CoE Leader at the c suite tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
